question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded `isNew()` returning `true` to force a blind INSERT of a ContractResult record with an attacker-chosen `consensusTimestamp`, bypassing any duplicate-check, thereby injecting a phantom transaction into the historical record and reordering the apparent sequence of contract executions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes `getId()` to return a `consensusTimestamp` value that collides with an existing legitimate record, causing the persistence layer to silently overwrite or shadow the original transaction history entry, effectively erasing a real contract execution from the historical view?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `toContractTransactionHash()` with a null or attacker-controlled `transactionHash` byte array, causing the resulting `ContractTransactionHash` record to map an incorrect hash to a legitimate `consensusTimestamp`, thereby corrupting the hash-to-timestamp index and making a real transaction unreachable by its hash?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that produces a `ContractResult` whose `transactionHash` collides with an existing entry in `contract_transaction_hash`, causing the hash-based lookup to return the attacker's record instead of the legitimate one, reorganizing which transaction is surfaced when queried by hash?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `contractId` field passed into `toContractTransactionHash()` so that `entityId` in the resulting `ContractTransactionHash` points to a different contract, causing the transaction history of that contract to be polluted with a foreign transaction entry?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `toContractTransactionHash()` to be called when `payerAccountId` is null, triggering a NullPointerException at `payerAccountId.getId()` that disrupts the ingestion pipeline and causes subsequent legitimate transactions to be dropped or reordered in the history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setBloom()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract execution that produces a bloom filter byte array of exactly 256 zero bytes, causing `setBloom()` to replace it with `ArrayUtils.EMPTY_BYTE_ARRAY`, thereby stripping bloom data from the historical record and making log-based transaction history queries return incorrect or empty results?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setBloom()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a bloom filter that is one byte short of 256 bytes (255 bytes of zeros), bypassing the `Arrays.equals(bloom, EMPTY_BLOOM)` check and persisting a near-empty bloom that causes false-positive log lookups, making unrelated transactions appear in filtered history views?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isNew()=true` invariant combined with a duplicate `consensusTimestamp` to cause a primary key constraint violation that rolls back a batch of legitimate transactions, creating gaps in the transaction history that make the ledger appear to have missing blocks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionIndex field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit an Ethereum transaction with a manipulated `transactionIndex` value that conflicts with an existing record at the same block, causing the REST API to return results in a different order than the actual consensus sequence, reorganizing the apparent transaction history for that block?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionNonce field)] Can an unprivileged external user replay a transaction with a manipulated `transactionNonce` that matches a previously used nonce for the same sender, causing the mirror node to store a duplicate nonce entry that confuses nonce-based transaction ordering and makes the sender's history appear to have a gap or reordering?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionResult field)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `ContractResult` to be persisted with a `transactionResult` code that misrepresents the actual outcome (e.g., SUCCESS instead of WRONG_NONCE), causing history consumers to believe a transaction succeeded when it did not, reorganizing the apparent success/failure sequence of contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (consensusTimestamp field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that is processed with a `consensusTimestamp` that is out of chronological order relative to existing records, causing the mirror node to insert a record that appears earlier in history than it actually occurred, reorganizing the apparent sequence of contract executions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (senderId field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call where the `senderId` EntityId is set to a different account than the actual caller, causing the transaction to appear in the history of a victim account and polluting that account's contract execution history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (payerAccountId field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `payerAccountId` field to associate a `ContractResult` with a victim account's ID, causing the victim's transaction history to show contract executions they never initiated, reorganizing the apparent attribution of historical transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (createdContractIds field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract creation that populates `createdContractIds` with IDs of pre-existing contracts, causing the history to falsely show those contracts as having been created by this transaction, reorganizing the apparent creation order of contracts in the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (errorMessage field)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract execution that produces an `errorMessage` matching the error message of a different, legitimate failed transaction, causing history consumers to conflate the two failures and misattribute the cause of historical transaction failures?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionHash field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose `transactionHash` is a 32-byte prefix collision with a longer hash of a legitimate transaction, causing the `contract_transaction_hash` index lookup to return the attacker's record first and shadow the legitimate transaction in hash-based history queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `ContractResult` to be stored with a `gasConsumed` value of null when it should be non-null, causing gas-based analytics over transaction history to produce incorrect ordering or aggregation results that misrepresent the sequence of resource-intensive operations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (functionParameters field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with `functionParameters` that are identical to those of a previous legitimate call, causing deduplication or caching logic in history consumers to return the attacker's result instead of the original, effectively replacing a historical record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (failedInitcode field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a failed contract creation with a `failedInitcode` that matches the initcode of a legitimate contract, causing the history to show the legitimate contract's deployment as having failed, reorganizing the apparent deployment history of that contract?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple transactions with the same `transactionHash` but different `consensusTimestamp` values, causing `toContractTransactionHash()` to insert multiple hash-to-timestamp mappings, making hash-based lookups return an unpredictable record and disrupting the canonical ordering of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionIndex field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with `transactionIndex=0` when another transaction already occupies index 0 in the same block, causing the REST API's block-ordered transaction list to show the attacker's transaction as the first in the block, reorganizing the apparent block-level transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ContractResult (consensusTimestamp field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nanosecond precision of `consensusTimestamp` to submit a transaction with a timestamp that is only 1 nanosecond after a legitimate transaction, causing the two records to appear adjacent in history in a different order than their actual network submission order?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isNew()=true` behavior to cause the ORM to always attempt an INSERT rather than an UPDATE, and by timing a submission to race with an in-flight legitimate transaction at the same `consensusTimestamp`, cause a race condition that results in one of the two records being silently dropped from history?
